assumptions_used: LFP CAM specific energy estimated at 3-6 kWh/kg based on NMC analog (Ahmed et al. 2017) — no published LFP-specific industrial data exists [CLM-028]
GHG reduction percentages (55-70% by 2030, 80-90% by 2035) based on Italy NECP grid trajectory — actual grid decarbonisation may differ
DT performance benchmarks (10-50% scrap reduction, 12-15% energy savings) based on cross-industry analogs — not yet validated for LFP CAM specifically
Surrogate model real-time inference <1 second — feasibility dependent on model complexity and hardware selection
Edge computing latency <100ms — dependent on hardware architecture and network design
open_issues: Chinese-language CNIPA patent search not completed — residual risk that unpublished Chinese DT implementations exist for LFP CAM
LFP-specific calcination energy consumption data not available — GHG estimates rely on analog estimation with +/-30% uncertainty
Long-term Raman probe reliability at 600-700 C not demonstrated — at-line fallback strategy documented but in-line claim is qualified
Exact production capacity not specified — TRL end targets may vary with scale
FREYR Vaasa status should be re-checked closer to submission — competitive positioning may shift
Specific DT software platform selection (commercial vs open-source) not yet decided — affects AspenTech patent risk [SRC-PAT-011]
Feasibility study details for plant design, mass/energy balances need to be referenced once available
novelty_anchors_used: NOV-001 (score 8): First DT for LFP CAM powder synthesis — lead anchor
NOV-002 (score 7): First fully electrified LFP CAM production plant in Europe
NOV-003 (score 7): First ISO 23247 + multi-scale modelling for chemical manufacturing
NOV-005 (score 6): First QbD/PAT framework for battery CAM
gaps_addressed: GAP-001: No DT for LFP CAM synthesis
GAP-002: No European LFP CAM production
GAP-003: Electrified LFP calcination not validated at production scale
GAP-004: No ISO 23247 for chemical powder manufacturing
GAP-005: No QbD/PAT for battery CAM
